items.title: New era of trade volatility: What the court’s decision and Trump’s tariff pivot mean for commodities
items.description: The U.S. Supreme Court blocked Donald Trump’s sweeping tariffs under IEEPA, but his swift 15% global levy revived uncertainty. The ruling jolted global trade dynamics, pressured the dollar, lifted gold and silver, and heightened volatility across energy and base metals markets.

items.title: Titan Company shines, Britannia Industries steadies: consumer stocks back in play
items.description: India’s consumer sector showed gradual recovery in 3QFY26, with 17% revenue and 15% EBITDA growth aided by low base, stable input costs and improving sentiment. Food and staples led gains, while discretionary recovery remained uneven. Titan Company and Britannia Industries delivered strong quarterly performances.

items.title: Anthropic hack puts IT stock pack on slide row in February; Nifty IT's 19% fall worst since 2008 crisis
items.description: The Nifty IT index plunged 19.5% in February, its steepest monthly drop since 2008, as AI disruption fears and new tools from Anthropic triggered heavy selling. Tech stocks erased Rs 5.7 lakh crore in market value, with Coforge and LTIMindtree among the worst hit.

items.title: Average lending rates on new loans firm up
items.description: January saw Mumbai's new rupee-loan rates climb, propelled mainly by state-owned lenders. By contrast, rates on existing loans dipped slightly and deposit rates declined a touch. The move unfolds in the RBI's policy backdrop, after its prior repo-rate reduction.

items.title: Can AI beat a great investor? Here's what billionaire Howard Marks says
items.description: Howard Marks believes AI possesses many investor qualities like data processing and unemotional decision-making, but lacks crucial human elements like "skin in the game" and intuitive risk assessment. While acknowledging AI's rapid advancement and potential, he advises a cautious, selective approach to investing in the technology, warning against going all-in.

items.title: Bad Memories of Another Day for Lenders as Bihar Moves to Regulate Microloans
items.description: Bihar's new microfinance law could stall the sector's rebound. The state assembly passed a bill regulating money lending, a move that may push up loan defaults, echoing issues seen in other states. Lenders are prioritizing borrower engagement to curb repayment delays, and the industry aims to reassure borrowers while safeguarding asset quality.

items.title: IIFL Home Finance inks $300 million loan pact with ADB
items.description: IIFL Home Finance has secured a $300 million loan from the Asian Development Bank to expand affordable housing lending for low-income families, with a special emphasis on women. A portion of the funds will back green-certified, energy-efficient homes, helping address India's substantial housing shortage and extending homeownership to underserved communities nationwide.

items.title: Sensex, Nifty singed at the end of a fiery February
items.description: Indian equities closed February on a weaker note as the Sensex and Nifty slipped, led by a sharp drop in IT shares amid concerns about AI-driven disruptions. Foreign institutional buyers returned, but domestic institutional purchases slowed. Geopolitical tensions and rich valuations kept risk sentiment under pressure, with analysts forecasting a continued uneasy tone in the near term.

items.title: Shipping Corporation fined: NSE, BSE impose Rs 5.42 lakh penalty each for Sebi norm violation
items.description: BSE and National Stock Exchange fined Shipping Corporation of India Rs 5.42 lakh each for violating Sebi’s board composition norms. The PSU said operations remain unaffected, even as it reported sharp profit growth and strong stock performance.

items.title: Concurrent Gainers: 11 stocks made gains for 5 days in a row
items.description: In the five trading sessions ending February 27, the Sensex declined by 1.84%, or 1,528 points, closing at 81,287 and posting losses in three of the five sessions. Despite the overall downturn during this period, about 11 stocks from the BSE 500 index managed to gain in each of the five consecutive sessions from February 23 to 27. (Data Source: ACE Equity)

items.title: BEL announces record date for interim dividend of Rs 1.95 per share
items.description: Bharat Electronics Limited announced an interim dividend of Rs 1.95 per share for FY26, with a record date of March 5. The company reported 21% YoY profit growth and robust revenue, with shares up 74% in the past year.
